title: Harden adoption readiness external approval boundaries

sources.reason: Defines existing readiness fixtures and regression expectations.
Defines the template's external review boundary.
Defines the template's security reporting boundary.

problemStatement: Adoption readiness can accept missing or stale template approval boundaries because it only checks one old placeholder spelling and skips absent CODEOWNERS or SECURITY.md files.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: current SECURITY.md template boundary fails adopted readiness
configured owner and private reporting channel pass readiness

riskAssessment.reason: The checker must reject incomplete adopter configuration without rejecting the template-maintenance exemption path.

executionDecision.reason: The readiness contract is explicit and covered by local regression tests.

preReviewWarnings: Review that template-maintenance exemptions remain explicit and adopted repositories fail closed on absent or placeholder approval boundaries.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Adopted readiness fails when CODEOWNERS or SECURITY.md is missing.
All current template placeholders and stale placeholder variants are rejected.
Configured owner rules and private vulnerability reporting satisfy the static boundary check.
Template-maintenance exemption behavior remains explicit and regression-tested.
guidelines: Fail closed when external approval evidence is absent.
Accept full Contract hashes and their stable prefixes consistently across checkpoint gates.

intent.problem: A copied template can be reported as adoption-ready even when the external human approval and security reporting boundaries are absent or still template placeholders.
constraints: Reject missing and placeholder adopter configuration fail-closed.
intent.rationale: Readiness must not self-declare trust where the repository and platform identity boundary has not been configured.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly confirmed the remediation loop; the complexity ceiling change records the required test and implementation growth.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
